title: Unknown JSON, Runtime Validators, Type Guards, Errors, and Schema Evolution · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds strict unknown-to-domain validation pipeline from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of strict unknown-to-domain validation pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Treat parsed external JSON as unknown and prevent it from entering domain or presentation code before validation.
Check non-null plain-enough objects, own keys, arrays, every member, primitive finiteness, bounds, and nesting explicitly.
Return a discriminated success or structured failure with path, expected shape, observed class, and bounded safe detail.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2164 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile unknown payload fixtures with deep validation, structured failures, and exact optional meaning; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat parsed external JSON as unknown and prevent it from entering domain or presentation code before validation.

What must be established before implementation?
